changes_made: Changed 'k8s_cluster_multi_zone_deployment_enabled' to 'k8s_cluster_multi_zone_enabled' for brevity and clarity
Changed 'k8s_persistent_volume_multi_zone_replication' to 'k8s_persistentvolume_multi_zone_replication' to match resource naming conventions
review_notes: The functions map well to the ISO27001 control A.8.14 by addressing redundancy through multi-zone deployments and configurations. Naming conventions were mostly followed, but minor adjustments were made for consistency and clarity. The functions are technically accurate and align with Kubernetes capabilities for redundancy.
naming_improvements: Use 'persistentvolume' instead of 'persistent_volume' for resource name consistency
title: Redundancy of information processing facilities
step1_reasoning: The ISO27001 control A.8.14 focuses on redundancy of information processing facilities, which can be mapped to Kubernetes by ensuring that workloads and data are distributed across multiple zones or nodes to prevent single points of failure. This involves configuring clusters to span multiple availability zones, using pod anti-affinity rules to distribute pods across nodes, enabling node autoscaling for resilience, and ensuring persistent volumes are replicated across zones.
